title: Cloudflare Protected Admin Access — deny-by-default Cloudflare Access in front of admin/preview hosts
description: A platform engineer exposes an admin console or a preview environment on a public hostname. Hulumi's Edge Platform (v1.3.2) provides ProtectedAdminHostname, an optional Cloudflare Access wrapper that puts a deny-by-default Access policy in front of the admin/preview host and requires an explicit allow-list of identities (email, email domain, Access group ID, or IdP ID). Target end state: no admin/preview host is reachable without passing Cloudflare Access, and the allowed-identity set is explicit and minimal.
actors: Authorized Operator (trusted; reaches admin via Access)
External Attacker (untrusted; attempts direct admin-host access)
Contributor (semi-trusted; may set an over-broad allow-list)
assets: Cloudflare Access application and policy configuration
Allowed-identity list (email / domain / Access group / IdP)
The admin application and its data

stride.name: Direct admin-host access without identity
stride.description: An attacker reaches the admin or preview host directly because no identity gate exists in front of it. Mitigation: ProtectedAdminHostname wraps the host in a Cloudflare Access application with a deny-by-default policy, so every request must pass Access before reaching the host.

stride.name: Over-broad allow-list grants unintended access
stride.description: The allow-list is set to a whole domain (or left effectively open), letting more identities through than intended. Mitigation: ProtectedAdminHostname requires explicit allowed identities (email, email domain, Access group ID, or IdP ID) with no wildcard-all default, and deny-by-default for everything else.

stride.name: Admin host discovered and information-disclosed
stride.description: The admin/preview hostname is discovered (DNS history, link leak, search indexing); without Access in front, an attacker can enumerate or read admin surfaces. Mitigation: the Access gate sits before the admin/preview host so discovery alone does not yield access.

stride.name: Admin access not attributable
stride.description: Admin access without an audit trail cannot be attributed during incident response. Mitigation: Cloudflare Access provides an authentication/authorization audit trail where the plan supports it; the component documents the audit dependency in its outputs.

stride.name: Raw Cloudflare Access resource misconfigured beside Hulumi
stride.description: A raw Cloudflare Access application/policy created next to Hulumi components ships with a permissive or empty policy. Mitigation: prefer ProtectedAdminHostname's typed deny-by-default shape; raw permissive Access configuration is an anti-pattern surfaced by review and the Cloudflare hardening guidance.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.ProtectedAdminHostname
recommendedComponents.rationale: Optional Cloudflare Access wrapper for admin/preview hosts: deny-by-default Access policy with an explicit allowed-identity list and no wildcard-all default.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.PublicHostname
recommendedComponents.rationale: The admin/preview hostname should still be a proxied PublicHostname so the Access gate and edge controls sit in front of it rather than a DNS-only record exposing the origin.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.cloudflare.HulumiCloudflareHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (Edge Platform; rule IDs stable)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Preview-time guardrails that keep the admin hostname proxied and origin-restricted so the Access gate cannot be bypassed at the network layer.
